title: China GP fallout: paddock turns attention to strategy and penalties
caption: Paddock discussions
body:
In the aftermath of the Chinese Grand Prix, attention across the paddock has shifted to the aggressive strategies and the penalty accumulation that defined the weekend. While Ferrari executed a flawless offset strategy to convert Väisänen's P8 start into a dominant victory, other teams were left deeply analysing what they lost — and how to stop it happening again in Suzuka.

The stewards were kept busy throughout the Shanghai weekend. Ferrari's second driver, Afonso Cashinho, received penalties in both the sprint and the main grand prix, accumulating six penalty points on his FIA racing licence along with a formal warning inside just two race weekends — a pace of disciplinary escalation that has left team principal Randy Belacik with no choice but to address the situation publicly. Cashinho's ability to manage high-pressure racecraft without costing himself points and positions has become the most urgent internal question at Maranello.

Meanwhile, the strategic battlefield highlighted the sheer complexity of mastering the Shanghai International Circuit. Red Bull's call to pit Jason Roy under the opening safety car was bold in theory but handed the whole strategic initiative to Ferrari, who had pre-loaded their offset with Väisänen on hard compounds. Quarter Kevu managed his VCARB machinery with the calm intelligence of a defending champion to claim second, while the circuit's long, high-deg second sector proved punishing for any team running on the wrong compound at the wrong moment.

As the freight departs for Suzuka, the data from Shanghai's extended hard-tyre stints and frantic pit-stop timing windows will be studied intensively. Japan's high-speed load profile is expected to reward aerodynamic efficiency and mechanical balance more honestly than Australia or China, and several teams are understood to be bringing targeted updates for the third round. The question of whether anyone can close the gap to Ferrari is the one everyone in the paddock is working towards answering.
